name: Xu Shu (Yuanzhi)
page_title: Xu Shu (Yuanzhi) 徐庶 (元直) [Shu, Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Xu Shu’s mother was captured by Cao Cao. Because of this, Xu Shu, who did wish to serve Liu Bei, parted ways with Liu Bei and took up service with Cao Cao. [SGZ/ZZTJ]
	Xu Shu’s mother did not commit suicide. [SGZ]

sections.short-biography: Told Liu Bei about Zhuge Liang. Joined Cao Cao after hearing his mother was captured.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	徐庶 (元直)


	 Simplified Chinese 
	徐庶 (元直)


	 Pronunciation 
	Xu2 Shu4 (Yuan2zhi2) 


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Xú Shù (Yuánzhí)


	 Wade-Giles 
	Hsü Shu (Yüan-chih)

	 Other Names 
	Shan Fu, Dan Fu, Xu Zhe [Destiny of an Emperor]

	 Name Notes 
	Referenced as Shan Fu (單福), or Dan Fu, in the novel, which may have been a mistranslation of “本單家子” in his Sanguozhi biography (“son of Shan” vs “son of poor” family).

sections.biography-source-undefined:
Xu Shu, styled Yuanzhi, was from Yingchuan commandery. He was born into a poor family, and his original name was Xu Fu.

When he was young, he enjoyed being chivalrous and he was good at swordsmanship. During the end of the Zhongping era (184–189), he took revenge for someone; it is implied he killed. He attempted to escape the law by putting chalk on his face and unloosening his hair to disguise himself, but was caught by an official. The official interrogated him about his identity, but Xu Shu said nothing. The official tied him to a vehicle, threatened to dismember him publicly, and beat drums in the city center to summon the people and have them identify Xu Shu. No one was willing to identify Xu Shu, and some of his friends were able to free him from the official. Xu Shu was so touched by this that he stopped living a life of fighting and instead devoted himself to scholarly studies. It is likely around this time that Xu Shu changed his name from Xu Fu.

After that, Xu Shu went to a school, but many people avoided associating with him because of his past killing. Xu Shu resolved to be more humble, waking up early each day to sweep the floors clean all by himself, while doing his studies. By reading classical works, he understood them greatly, and he was able to articulate his points and logic very well. This made him a few friends, among them Shi Tao.

During the Chuping era (190–193), Xu Shu and Shi Tao fled south to Jing province to escape warfare. When Zhuge Liang moved into Jing, he and Xu Shu became close, wandering around to study. At the time, Zhuge Liang was not famous, but claimed that he had similar talent to the historical figures Guan Zhong and Yue Yi. Many people did not believe him, but Xu Shu was one of the few who said that it was true.

When Liu Bei arrived in Xinye in 201, it was around that time Xu Shu met him. Liu Bei treated him well and the two men grew close. Xu Shu became a sort of an informal advisor to Liu Bei, attempting to help him achieve hegemony. He told Liu Bei about Zhuge Liang, saying, “Zhuge Kongming is a Sleeping Dragon. General, how can you expect to meet him?” Liu Bei said, “We should both make him come.” Xu Shu said, “That man can be seen, but he cannot be summoned. General, you ought to deign yourself to visit him and attend to him.” Liu Bei and Zhuge Liang promptly met and Zhuge Liang joined Liu Bei.

In 208, when Cao Cao got the surrender of Jing province, Liu Bei led a mass exodus south with those that did not want to serve under Cao Cao. Xu Shu followed Liu Bei until he heard that his mother was captured in the north, and at that point, he told Liu Bei he was resigning to join Cao Cao, stating, “I originally wanted to work with you to undertake the cause of a hegemon king, using this heart of mine. Now that I have lost my elderly mother, my heart is in turmoil and cannot benefit you in your deeds. Please let me depart from here.” Liu Bei let him go.

Xu Shu would serve Cao Cao, Cao Pi, and Cao Rui. He would hold government posts, but did nothing especially notable. In one campaign Zhuge Liang launched against Wei, he bemoaned Xu Shu not being realized to his full potential by the Wei government after hearing about the rank Xu Shu held. Xu Shu would die of illness a few years later.

full_text:
Xu Shu (Yuanzhi) 徐庶 (元直)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Xu Shu (徐庶), styledYuanzhi (元直). Told Liu Bei about Zhuge Liang. Joined Cao Cao after hearing his mother was captured. Xu Shu was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom and the Wei Kingdom. Xu Shu lived during the Later Han Dynasty and Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.
Return to theThree Kingdoms Encyclopediaor browse theEncyclopedia Directory.
